titleStmt.title: Telegram from Superintendent of Police, Kingstown to Mabel FitzGerald, 10 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Superintendent of Police, Kingstown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Telegram from police superintendent, Kingstown to Mabel FitzGerald, born Mabel Washington McConnell (1884–1958), wife of Irish revolutionary Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947). In the telegram, Mabel is informed that her husband is being held at Kingstown Police Station where she may visit him and bring refreshments.Mabel was a former governess in London. She later vigorously canvassed her former acquaintances in London's liberal circles for support for Francis Sheehy Skeffington (1878-1916) during his hunger strike in 1915. Both Mabel and Desmond would serve in the GPO garrison during Easter Week.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
POST OFFICE TELEGRAPHS
Office of Origin and Service Instructions
HMS Kingstown Handed in at 8.55 Received here at 9.45 TO Mrs FitzGerald, 2 Loretto Villas, Bray Husband at Kingstown Police station
can be seen send refreshment Supt of Police Kingstown
